title: The kubectl command-line tool — Extending kubectl with plugins
text:
You can extend
kubectl
with
plugins
that add new
sub-commands. Plugins are standalone binaries that follow the
kubectl-<plugin-name>
naming convention.
The Kubernetes community maintains many plugins, and you can manage them with the
Krew
plugin manager.

The
kubectl
tool supports a version skew of plus-or-minus one minor version relative to the cluster's
control plane. For example,
kubectl
v1.32 works with control planes at v1.31, v1.32, and v1.33.
Using a compatible version avoids unexpected behavior. See the
version skew policy
for details.
